eadheader.eadid: PUBLIC "-//University of California, Los Angeles::Library::UCLA Library Special Collections//TEXT(US::CLU-SC::1422::Juana de Laban papers)//EN" "laba1422.xml"

titlestmt.titleproper: Finding Aid for the Juana de Laban papers, 1830-1975
titlestmt.author: Processed by Yasmin Damshenas in the Center for Primary Research and Training (CFPRT), with assistance from Megan Fraser, July 2011; machine-readable finding aid created by Caroline Cubé.

address.addressline: Room A1713, Charles E. Young Research Library

publicationstmt.p: The Regents of the University of California. All rights reserved.

profiledesc.creation: Machine-readable finding aid derived from MS Word document, encoding added via Notetab Pro. Date of source: August 2011. Supplementary encoding and revision by Caroline Cubé using Notetab Pro.
profiledesc.descrules: Finding aid prepared using Describing Archives: a Content Standard

did.head: Descriptive Summary
did.unittitle: Juana de Laban papers
did.abstract: Juana de Laban (1910-1978) was the director of graduate studies in theater at Baylor University, a distinguished professor at the University of Wisconsin, and served on the dance faculty at UCLA for nearly a decade. She was active in many educational and professional organizations, including the Sacred Dance Guild of America. The collection consists of books, pamphlets, dance programs, magazines and journals, as well as correspondence, typed drafts and carbon copies, photographs, clippings, notes, and sketches.
did.langmaterial: Finding aid is written in English.

repository.label: Repository
repository.corpname: University of California, Los Angeles. Library Special Collections.

did.physloc: Stored off-site at SRLF. Advance notice is required for access to the collection. Please contact UCLA Library Special Collections for paging information.

accessrestrict.head: Restrictions on Access
accessrestrict.p: Open for research. STORED OFF-SITE AT SRLF. Advance notice is required for access to the collection. Please contact UCLA Library Special Collections for paging information.

userestrict.head: Restrictions on Use and Reproduction
userestrict.p: Property rights to the physical object belong to the UC Regents. Literary rights, including copyright, are retained by the creators and their heirs. It is the responsibility of the researcher to determine who holds the copyright and pursue the copyright owner or his or her heir for permission to publish where The UC Regents do not hold the copyright.

processinfo.head: Processing Note
processinfo.p: Processed by Yasmin Damshenas in the Center for Primary Research and Training (CFPRT), with assistance from Megan Fraser, July 2011.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: [Identification of item], Juana de Laban papers (Collection 1422). UCLA Library Special Collections, Charles E. Young Research Library, UCLA.

bioghist.head: Biography/History
bioghist.p: Juana de Laban was a notable figure in the dance world, having participated in the field as a dancer, teacher, choreographer, director, critic and scholar. Of Hungarian descent, Juana de Laban von Varalja was born on October 21, 1910 en route from Austria to Switzerland. She was the daughter of Rudolf von Laban, the inventor of the system of Labanotation, a groundbreaking method of notating and interpreting human movement. De Laban studied with the Institute of Choreographics in Berlin and the Royal Hungarian Academy in Budapest. She spent time working for European film companies TERRA and URA and became known for her interpretation of native Hungarian dances. With the start of World War II, de Laban relocated to Texas and eventually received her master's and doctoral degrees in dramatic theory and criticism from Yale University. She is said to be the recipient of the first PhD ever granted in the U.S. for the study of dance criticism. De Laban became director of graduate studies in theater at Baylor University. She went on to serve on the dance faculty at UCLA for eight years, during which time she started a free lecture series, worked to build the library in the area of dance history and aesthetics, and helped Library Special Collections acquire the Ruth St. Denis papers. She was active in many educational and professional organizations, including the Committee on Research in Dance, Sacred Dance Guild of America and the Dallas Theater Center. In the fall of 1971, de Laban moved back to Texas as director of the graduate program in dance at Southern Methodist University. In 1973 she relocated to Temple, Texas where she later died on July 4, 1978. Her publications include Dance Notation (c. 1946).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Content
scopecontent.p: Collection consists of books, pamphlets, dance programs, magazines and journals, as well as correspondence, typed drafts and carbon copies, photographs, clippings, notes, and sketches. Languages in this collection include English, Hungarian, German, Japanese and French.

arrangement.head: Organization and Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials are organized into two boxes. Box 1 contains publications. Box 2 contains additional publications, as well as, photographs, manuscripts, notes, clippings, and drawings.

controlaccess.head: Indexing Terms
controlaccess.p: The following terms have been used to index the description of this collection in the library's online public access catalog.

did.unittitle: "All that strange & mysterious folk." Dance Perspectives 16.1, no.61.
Anderson, Jack (ed.). "Dance, the Dancer, and the Poem: An Anthology of Twentieth Century Poems." Dance Perspectives, no. 52
Aschengreen, Erik. "Beautiful Danger." Dance Perspectives, no.58
Baron, John H. "Les Fees des Forests de S. Germain Ballet de Cour, 1625." Dance Perspectives 16.2, no.62
Beiswanger, George. "Three Essays in Dance Aesthetics." Dance Perspectives, no.55
Blasis, M. Manuel complet de la danse. [France]: Librairie Encyclopedique
Blass, Ernst. Das Wesen der neuen Tanzkunst. Weimar: Erich Lichtenstein Verlag
Blum, Odette. "Dance in Ghana." Dance Perspectives, no. 56
Books & Scores from the Bureau. New York: Dance Notation Bureau
Dance Magazine 38, no.8
Dancers of Bali. New York: Lou Dunetz and Paul Lovett Publishers
Duncan, Irma. Isadora Duncan: Pioneer in the Art of Dance. New York: New York Public Library
First Lady of the Dance. Los Angeles: Burns
Florenz, Karl. Japanische Dramen: Terakoya und Asagao. Leipzig: C.F. Amelangs Verlag
Gauguin, Paul. Noa Noa. Berlin: Verlag von Bruno Cassirer
János, Arany. Összes kÖltői művei. Budapest: Franklin-Tarsut

Kaposi, Edit (ed.). Táncművészeti Értesítő. Budapest: A Magyar Táncművészek SzÖvetségének [The Hungarian Dancers Association].Vol. 2.
Kaposi, Edit (ed.). Táncművészeti értesítő. Budapest: A Magyar Táncművészek SzÖvetségének [The Hungarian Dancers Association].Vol. 2.

Koegler, Horst. "In the Shadow of the Swastika." Dance Perspectives, no. 57
de Laban, Juana. "Dance Notation." Dance Index 5, no. 4-5 (1946). New York: Dance Index-Ballet Caravan.

scopecontent.p: (Has a sheet of notes inserted. This issue also includes an article by Juana de Laban's father, Rudolf von Laban.)

did.unittitle: Laban, Rudolf. Choreutics. London: Macdonald and Evans
Leabo, Karl (ed.). Martha Graham Dance Company. New York: The Martha Graham Center for Contemporary Dance
Morgan, Barbara. Martha Graham: Sixteen Dances in Photographs. New York: Duell, Sloan & Pearce
New York City Ballet Program. New York: New York Center of Music & Drama
New York City Ballet Program. [s.l.]: [s.n.]
Roland Petit's Ballets de Paris in Carmen and Other Ballets. New York: Benj. Weitzner
Stepanov, V.I. Alphabet of Movements. Cambridge: Golden Head Press

note.p: (Number 6 of 175 copies printed for sale in 1958)

did.unittitle: Schlundt, Christena, L. Ruth St. Denis & Ted Shawn. New York: New York Public Library
Schlundt, Christena L. Ted Shawn and his men dancers. New York: New York Public Library

scopecontent.p: (Contains letter from Schlundt to de Laban, dated October 18, 1963.)

did.unittitle: Shawn, Ted. Ruth St. Denis: Pioneer & Prophet, Volumes I and II. San Francisco: John Henry Nash

note.p: (This is the printer's copy, only 350 copies were printed.)

did.unittitle: Van Tuyl, Marian. Extensions of Dance. San Francisco: Impulse Publications
Williams, Peter (ed.). London's Festival Ballet. New York: Artcraft Litho and Printing Co., Inc.
Photograph of Ruth St. Denis.
"Magyar Dances" aka "Hungarian Dances" draft and notes by Juana de Laban.

scopecontent.p: (In English, Hungarian and German, includes sketch, dance notations, glossary, discography for records, correspondence, press clippings, musical scores, flyers, book titled Magyar Táncok from ca. 1933)
